openmeter/customer/service/hooks.purpose: Lifecycle hook implementations for the customer domain — entitlement validation pre-delete guard (entitlementvalidator.go) and subject-to-customer auto-provisioning (subjectcustomer.go). New hooks integrate with models.ServiceHook[T] registry without coupling the customer package to callers.

patterns.name: ServiceHook type alias pair
patterns.description: Each hook file declares a public type alias for the interface and a Noop alias. The concrete struct embeds the Noop so only overridden methods need implementing.

patterns.name: Config struct + Validate() + constructor
patterns.description: Every hook is constructed via a Config struct with a Validate() error method. Constructor calls Validate() first, nil-dependency checks are mandatory inside Validate.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Every concrete hook struct declares a package-level var _ assertion to verify interface satisfaction at compile time.

patterns.name: OTel tracing in hook methods
patterns.description: Non-trivial hook methods start a span via tracer.Start, record errors with span.RecordError + span.SetStatus, and defer span.End. The tracer is injected through the Config struct.

patterns.name: Loop-prevention via context key
patterns.description: When a hook calls back into customer.Service (UpdateCustomer/CreateCustomer), it wraps ctx with subjectservicehooks.NewContextWithSkipSubjectCustomer(ctx) to break re-entrant hook cycles.

patterns.name: models.GenericError types for domain errors
patterns.description: Validation failures return models.NewGenericValidationError; not-found returns models.NewGenericNotFoundError; conflicts return models.NewGenericConflictError; precondition failures return models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError. Never return raw errors for domain conditions.
patterns.example: return models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("customer has entitlements, please remove them before deleting the customer"))

patterns.name: Test env built from package constructors (no app/common)
patterns.description: Tests wire adapters and services directly (customeradapter.New, customerservice.New, subjectadapter.New) without importing app/common. testutils.InitPostgresDB provides the Ent client; eventbus.NewMock provides the publisher.
patterns.example:
db := testutils.InitPostgresDB(t)
customerAdapter, _ := customeradapter.New(customeradapter.Config{Client: db.EntDriver.Client(), Logger: logger})
customerService, _ := customerservice.New(customerservice.Config{Adapter: customerAdapter, Publisher: publisher})

key_file_guides.role: PreDelete hook for customer.Customer — blocks deletion when active entitlements exist. Minimal hook overriding only one method; all others fall through to the embedded Noop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses clock.Now() for entitlement snapshot time; always pass the caller's ctx, never context.Background().

key_file_guides.role: PostCreate/PostUpdate/PostDelete hook for subject.Subject — auto-provisions or updates a matching Customer. Houses CustomerProvisioner (reusable create/update logic) and EnsureStripeCustomer (billing override sync).
key_file_guides.watch_for: getCustomerForSubject tries both usage-attribution lookup and key lookup. EnsureCustomer handles keyConflict by omitting the key on create. CmpSubjectCustomer determines whether an update is needed — extend carefully to avoid spurious updates.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests for CustomerProvisioner — exercises create, update, and conflict scenarios against a real Postgres instance via testutils.InitPostgresDB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestEnv is local to this package and independent from app/common. Tests call env.DBSchemaMigrate(t) before using the DB. Use t.Context(), not context.Background().

anti_patterns: Calling customer.Service from a hook without subjectservicehooks.NewContextWithSkipSubjectCustomer(ctx) — causes infinite re-entrant hook invocations.
Returning raw fmt.Errorf for domain conditions (not-found, conflict, validation) — callers and HTTP encoders depend on models.Generic* error types.
Importing app/common in test files — breaks isolation and can introduce import cycles; build test deps from package constructors directly.
Embedding a non-Noop base struct — hooks must embed the Noop alias so future interface method additions don't break compilation.
Using context.Background() or context.TODO() inside hook methods — always propagate the caller's ctx to maintain tracing and cancellation.

decisions.decision: Hooks embed models.NoopServiceHook[T] rather than implementing all methods.
decisions.rationale: The ServiceHook interface grows as new lifecycle events are added; embedding Noop provides safe defaults so existing hooks don't break on interface expansion.

decisions.decision: CustomerProvisioner is a separate struct from the hook.
decisions.rationale: EnsureCustomer and EnsureStripeCustomer are reused from outside the hook (e.g. app/stripe provisioning flows), so extracting them into a standalone CustomerProvisioner avoids duplication without tying the logic to the subject lifecycle.

Subject creation/update should not fail if customer provisioning is non-critical in some deployment modes; the flag lets the caller degrade gracefully (warn + continue) rather than surfacing the error.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new PreDelete hook that checks billing conditions before customer deletion
